_comment: Low-load BLE profile for dense RF environments (50-100+ advertisers).
_comment_why: Reduces the advertising-report flood that can wedge software controllers such as the nRF52840 Zephyr hci_usb dongle. Combines passive scanning (no SCAN_REQ TX), BlueZ duplicate filtering, and AdvertisementMonitor or_patterns so filtering happens below the Python layer.
_comment_scanning_mode: 'passive' stops the gateway emitting SCAN_REQ packets (less controller TX) and pushes filtering to BlueZ. Requires or_patterns (auto-built from the whitelists below). Omit this key, or set 'active', for the original behavior.
_comment_duplicate_filtering: Suppress duplicate advertisement report data at BlueZ (fewer host-ward reports). Applies to active scanning; passive scanning dedups inherently. Default true.
_comment_filters: These two whitelists are used BOTH for app-level filtering AND to auto-build the BlueZ or_patterns (service UUID -> AD type 0x07, manufacturer ID -> AD type 0xFF). A report matching EITHER pattern is delivered, so the target's connectable 1M set (carries the service UUID) and its non-connectable Coded-PHY set (carries only manufacturer 0xF0F0) are both received.
